name: waitlist_signups_with_referral_rate
definition: Percentage of waitlist signups who complete at least one referral share (click tracked on share button or unique referral link generated).
target_hint: 25–40% is healthy for a well-designed referral loop. <15% means the share UX or reward is broken.

name: waitlist_viral_coefficient
definition: Average number of new signups generated per existing signup via referral links (K-factor). Calculated as (referrals sent × conversion rate on referral link).
target_hint: K > 0.4 indicates organic growth potential; K > 1.0 means exponential waitlist growth. Below 0.2, redesign the loop before scaling traffic.

name: waitlist_to_activation_rate
definition: Percentage of waitlist signups who complete onboarding (first meaningful action) within 7 days of launch access.
target_hint: 40–60% for B2B SaaS with a clear ICP. <25% suggests waitlist attracted wrong audience or onboarding fails message match.

name: waitlist_growth_week_over_week
definition: Net new waitlist signups divided by total signups at start of week, expressed as a percentage.
target_hint: 15–30% WoW during active hype phase. Flat or declining for 2+ weeks means the hype engine needs a new wave (milestone, teaser, cohort invite).

name: referral_driven_signup_share
definition: Percentage of total waitlist signups attributed to referral links (not direct or paid traffic).
target_hint: 35–55% referral share indicates a working loop. <20% means you're relying on founder hustle, not systemized hype.
